why did the ancient egyptians call their land kemet, or black land? why did the ancient egyptians call their land kemet, or black land? why did the ancient egyptians call their land kemet, or black land? why did the ancient egyptians call their land kemet, or black land? why did the ancient egyptians call their land kemet, or black land? The ancient Egyptians referred to their land as Kemet, or the "Black Land," due to its rich, fertile soil. This fertile land arose from the annual inundation of the Nile River, which deposited dark, nutrient-rich silt across the plains. The term "Kemet" comes from the ancient Egyptian word "km," which means "black." This soil contrasted with the "Desert of Sinai," or "Deshret," which was known as the "Red Land," referring to the reddish-brown sand of the desert surrounding the fertile river valley. The designation of the region around the Nile as the "Black Land
